tests.intent: The exact content-addressed selected-Mocha 300-frame replay, 320x200 resolution, warm boundary, measured range, command digest and deterministic frame sequence are present without substitutions or omissions.

Every frame has evaluator-owned monotonic wall-clock measurements and all measured frames expose transport, presentation, named retained-worker, R1 and R2 durations as finite nonnegative observations.
The report derives p50 and p95 latency, database, ORDS, payload and decode/blit statistics, effective FPS, R1/R2 timing and cursor totals from the complete measured raw sample population.
Each exercised statement family has an execution plan captured with ALLSTATS LAST runtime rows, starts, elapsed time and a plan hash after representative bound executions.
V$SQL observations retain redacted SQL identity, force-matching signature, parse calls and execution deltas for each family without bind values or connection secrets.
Bound SQL statement shapes and force-matching signatures remain invariant across all required poses and command classes while executions advance and hard-parse growth stays explicitly reported.
Every replay frame records exact response payload bytes and content hash, and measured payload distribution is reported from the corresponding raw observations.
Database and renderer stage instrumentation exists only in retained evaluator evidence and is absent from response bodies, response schemas and public decompressed payload keys.
Samples, plans, V$SQL snapshots, payload ledger and replay identity are separately retained, nonempty, content-addressed raw artifacts with safe relative paths and byte counts.
A complete content-addressed baseline report references the raw artifact manifest and records environment and tool identities without claiming a machine-independent threshold.
Recursive evidence and artifact scanning rejects credentials, authorization material, endpoints, wallet data, bind values, session identifiers and other secret-bearing content.
Evidence records evaluator ownership of wall timing, atomic publication, UTC capture bounds and success only after all raw and report artifacts were closed and hashed.
Missing production driver, incomplete replay, absent database evidence, synthetic or dry-run markers, unreadable artifacts and any validation exception prevent a PASS result.
